nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Somerville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Graveron-Sémerville in Eure (Normandy), from the ancient Germanic personal name Sigimar (see Siemer ) + Old French ville ‘settlement’. The surname was taken to Scotland in the 12th century by William de Somerville, a retainer of David, Earl of Huntingdon, who became King David I of Scotland.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Somacháin (see Summers ) or Mag Shamradháin (see Somers ).

variants.name: Siemer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Helmuth, Otto, Theresia.
German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Siegmar, composed of the elements sigi ‘victory’ + mari, meri ‘famous’.

variants.name: Summerville
meanings: English, Scottish (Lanarkshire), and Irish: variant of   Somerville  . This form of the surname is particularly common in Ireland.

variants.name: Simrell
meanings: Scottish: perhaps related to the Ulster nameSumeril, a variant of Somerville .

variants.name: Summerfield
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called with Old English sumor ‘summer’ + feld ‘field, open land’, denoting summer pasture or fields cultivated only in summer, such as Summerfield in Woodnesborough (Kent), Somerfield Court in Sellindge (Kent), and Summerfield in Marlborough (Wiltshire).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Sommerfeld  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Roger de Somerville was born in Wychnor, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom as the son of Roger I de Somerville and Edelina le Boteler. He married Maud Hamilton. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died about 1272, in Staffordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Roger de Somerville est né à Wychnor, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de Roger I de Somerville et Edelina le Boteler. Il a épousé Maud Hamilton. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé environ 1272, à Staffordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Roger de Somerville wurde in Wychnor, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Roger I de Somerville und Edelina le Boteler geboren. Er hat Maud Hamilton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb um 1272 in Staffordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Roger de Somerville è nato il nel Wychnor, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di Roger I de Somerville e Edelina le Boteler. Lui sposato Maud Hamilton. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì circa 1272, nel Staffordshire, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Roger de Somerville(은)는 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 윈이ch너에서 Roger I de Somerville(와)과 Edelina le Boteler의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Maud Hamilton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1272년 영국 스태퍼드실르에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Roger de Somerville родился в местности вичнор, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. Его родителей звали Roger I de Somerville и Edelina le Boteler. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud Hamilton. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер около 1272 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия.
